content.text: I’m mapping out NovaBridge’s current progress, risks, and Q3 plan from the source files to build your leadership briefing.

content.content:
Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress
HEL-246  | UAT environment: server provisioning      | Nadia Petrov      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | At Risk
HEL-247  | Performance benchmark: dashboard load     | Derek Okafor      | 3      | 2026-07-18 | Not Started
HEL-248  | GeoSync license renewal coordination      | Raj Patel         | 2      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress

input.content:
Project Helios — Health Briefing for NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: July 11, 2026 (Week of July 6 Status)
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership
Classification: Internal Confidential

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
--------------------
Overall Status: ON TRACK (minor risks)
Project Helios remains on track for October 1 launch, but faces several active blockers that require close management before the July 25 product demo and August UAT window. Q2 closed $20k over budget with $47k additional Q3 overrun projected. Three Q2 deliverables are overdue. Key technical defects around date normalization and large-file ingestion must be resolved to meet Meridian contract requirements for live-data demo.

2. CURRENT PROGRESS
-------------------
Milestone Progress (as of week of 2026-07-06):
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete - Target completion: 2026-07-18
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete - Target completion: 2026-07-22
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Q2 Deliverables Checklist:
[DONE] Requirements documentation v1.0, System architecture design, API gateway implementation, Development environment setup, Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module, Client dashboard v1
[OVERDUE - Needs Attention]
  - Integration test suite setup: Was due 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20
  - UAT environment provisioning: Was due 2026-07-01, blocked by Meridian IT access
  - Performance benchmarking report: Was due 2026-07-05, Owner: Derek Okafor

Sprint 14 (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18) Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Sprint 14 Task Status:
- HEL-241 Dashboard: data table - Fatima Al-Rashid - In Progress - Due 07-14
- HEL-242 Dashboard: chart rendering - Tomas Rivera - In Progress - Due 07-16
- HEL-243 Data ingestion: date format normalization - Amir Hassan - Blocked
- HEL-244 Integration test suite: auth flows - Kenji Watanabe - Done (4/4 PASS)
- HEL-245 Integration test suite: data pipeline - Kenji Watanabe - In Progress
- HEL-246 UAT environment: server provisioning - Nadia Petrov - At Risk
- HEL-247 Performance benchmark: dashboard load - Derek Okafor - Not Started
- HEL-248 GeoSync license renewal coordination - Raj Patel - In Progress - Due 07-14

QA Testing Log Sprint 14 (Kenji Watanabe):
Overall: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL, 3 not yet run
- Authentication Flows: 4/4 PASS
- Data Pipeline Batch Ingestion: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL
- Dashboard: 1/4 complete

Critical Open Defects:
- HEL-D-014 [HIGH]: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input. Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Owner Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15.
- HEL-D-015 [HIGH]: Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow and crash at ~210k records. Owner Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Architecture: System is stateless, scalable (PostgreSQL + S3-compatible storage, Apache Spark transformation engine 500k records/min target, React dashboard, OAuth 2.0, TLS 1.3 / AES-256).

3. KEY RISKS (Risk Register — Last reviewed 2026-07-07, Owner: Sarah Chen)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------
Active High-Priority Risks:

R-01: Meridian source data quality issues - Likelihood High, Impact High, Score 9 - Owner Amir Hassan - ACTIVE. Date format blocker confirmed. Mitigation: Meridian IT Lead Priya Kapoor committed to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.

R-02: GeoSync license not renewed before demo - Medium/High - Score 6 - Owner Raj Patel - ACTIVE. Legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14. If not renewed, $12k additional cost projected.

R-03: Dashboard performance on large datasets - Medium/High - Score 6 - Owner Derek Okafor - ACTIVE. Benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started. Concern raised in 07-08 client meeting. Target <2 sec for 100k records not yet validated.

R-04: UAT timeline slippage - High/Medium - Score 6 - Owner Sarah Chen - ACTIVE. 1-week extension already consumed. UAT now Aug 17-28. Further slippage >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

R-06: Meridian IT environment access delays - Medium/Medium - Score 4 - Owner Nadia Petrov - ACTIVE. Blocks overdue UAT environment provisioning. Meridian to share IT infra diagram due 2026-07-12 and provision UAT access by 2026-07-19.

Monitored Risks:
R-05: Key person dependency: Derek Okafor - Low/High - Score 3 - No backup tech lead identified
R-07: Scope creep from Meridian feature requests - 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08: Cloud cost overrun
R-09: Third-party API deprecation

4. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS (Q3 Roadmap Draft — Last updated 2026-07-06 - INTERNAL)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note: Roadmap is internal draft, not for client distribution.

July - COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION:
- Finalize data ingestion module: 2026-07-18
- Complete client dashboard v1: 2026-07-22
- Integration Test Sign-off: 2026-07-25 (Next major milestone)

July 25 - PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (Confirmed):
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data (James Whitfield emphasized NO mock data per contract Section 9.3)
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
- Dashboard visualization with real data
- Draft demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (Owner: Sarah Chen)

August 17-28 - USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING:
- Deploy to UAT environment
- Support Meridian user testing sessions
- 2 sprints budgeted for UAT feedback remediation
- Meridian provision of UAT environment access by 2026-07-19 expected

August-September - PERFORMANCE HARDENING:
- Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
- Ingestion throughput benchmarking: 500k records/min sustained
- External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

September - LAUNCH PREPARATION:
- Production environment setup and runbook documentation
- Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
- Final client training sessions
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01

Proposed Q3 feature additions (require change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

5. ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
--------------------------------------
1. BUDGET OVERRUN:
   Q2 Actual $322k vs Budget $302k = +$20k over. Drivers: Engineering +$14.2k (unplanned sprint in May), Subcontractors +$6.8k (GeoSync scoping), Cloud infra +$3.5k.
   Projected Q3 additional overruns: $12k GeoSync license if not renewed, $15k UAT extension (1 week), estimated total H2 overrun $47k.
   Per MSA Section 4.2, internal overruns cannot be passed to Meridian. CFO review scheduled 2026-07-18. Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

2. CRITICAL TECHNICAL DEFECTS DEMO BLOCKERS:
   HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015 are both HIGH severity and directly impact the July 25 demo requirement for live data ingestion. Date issue affects 40% of source data. Memory overflow blocks 500k ingestion target. Need tech lead focus and potential additional engineering support.

3. OVERDUE DELIVERABLES & AT-RISK TASKS:
   Three Q2 deliverables overdue; Sprint 14 has 1 blocked, 1 at-risk, 1 not started (performance benchmark). Performance benchmarking was due July 5 and is required to address client concerns.

4. THIRD-PARTY DEPENDENCY - GEOSYNC LICENSE:
   Resolution target July 14. Legal review needs expediting. If missed, demo cannot use mapping features and $12k penalty cost. Owner Derek Okafor (task HEL-248 Raj Patel).

5. MERIDIAN IT DEPENDENCY:
   UAT environment provisioning blocked. Waiting on infrastructure diagram (due July 12 from Priya Kapoor) and access provisioning (July 19). If Meridian misses these, integration test sign-off July 25 and UAT start Aug 17 are at risk.

6. KEY PERSON RISK:
   Derek Okafor is sole technical lead with no backup identified (R-05). He owns performance benchmarking (overdue/not started) and GeoSync renewal action. This concentrates risk ahead of demo and launch.

7. SCOPE CREEP & CONTRACTUAL PENALTY:
   3 informal feature requests logged from Meridian (monitored risk). Q3 roadmap includes 3 proposed additions not yet approved via change control. UAT slippage beyond 2 weeks triggers Section 7.1 penalty review; launch would move to Q4.

6. RECOMMENDATIONS FOR LEADERSHIP
---------------------------------
- Approve contingency for GeoSync license and expedite legal sign-off before July 14.
- Direct resourcing for HEL-D-015 memory overflow investigation - consider pairing Amir Hassan with additional backend support.
- Assign backup technical lead to mitigate R-05 and cover performance benchmarking (HEL-247) this week.
- Engage CFO on budget reconciliation plan; decide on use of $15k untouched contingency reserve for Q3 overruns.
- Escalate to Meridian sponsor (James Whitfield) if IT infra diagram not received by July 12.
- Hold change control line firm on Q3 feature additions until integration sign-off complete.
- Prepare decision tree: If UAT slips >2 weeks, assess impact of Q4 launch and Section 7.1 penalties.

7. NEXT KEY DATES
-----------------
2026-07-12: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due
2026-07-14: GeoSync license renewal deadline
2026-07-15: Meridian clean sample data due (unblocks HEL-243 / HEL-D-014)
2026-07-18: Data ingestion complete target; Draft demo agenda due; CFO review; Performance benchmark due
2026-07-19: Meridian UAT access provisioning target
2026-07-20: Integration test suite setup rescheduled due; Budget reconciliation plan due
2026-07-22: Client dashboard v1 target
2026-07-25: Integration Test Sign-off + Product Demo for Meridian stakeholders
2026-08-17 to 08-28: UAT window
2026-10-01: Target Launch

---
Sources: project_helios_status.txt, risk_register.txt, q3_roadmap_draft.txt, q2_deliverables_checklist.txt, meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt, helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt, sprint_14_task_assignments.txt, budget_vs_actuals_q2.xlsx
Prepared by: Project Health Briefing Generator

4. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS (Q3 Roadmap Draft — Last updated 2026-07-06 - INTERNAL)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note: Roadmap is internal draft, not for client distribution.

July - COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION:
- Finalize data ingestion module: 2026-07-18
- Complete client dashboard v1: 2026-07-22
- Integration Test Sign-off: 2026-07-25 (Next major milestone)

July 25 - PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (Confirmed):
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data (James Whitfield emphasized NO mock data per contract Section 9.3)
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
- Dashboard visualization with real data
- Draft demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (Owner: Sarah Chen)

August 17-28 - USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING:
- Deploy to UAT environment
- Support Meridian user testing sessions
- 2 sprints budgeted for UAT feedback remediation
- Meridian provision of UAT environment access by 2026-07-19 expected

August-September - PERFORMANCE HARDENING:
- Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
- Ingestion throughput benchmarking: 500k records/min sustained
- External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

September - LAUNCH PREPARATION:
- Production environment setup and runbook documentation
- Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
- Final client training sessions
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01

Proposed Q3 feature additions (require change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

5. ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
--------------------------------------
1. BUDGET OVERRUN:
   Q2 Actual $322k vs Budget $302k = +$20k over. Drivers: Engineering +$14.2k (unplanned sprint in May), Subcontractors +$6.8k (GeoSync scoping), Cloud infra +$3.5k.
   Projected Q3 additional overruns: $12k GeoSync license if not renewed, $15k UAT extension (1 week), estimated total H2 overrun $47k.
   Per MSA Section 4.2, internal overruns cannot be passed to Meridian. CFO review scheduled 2026-07-18. Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

2. CRITICAL TECHNICAL DEFECTS DEMO BLOCKERS:
   HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015 are both HIGH severity and directly impact the July 25 demo requirement for live data ingestion. Date issue affects 40% of source data. Memory overflow blocks 500k ingestion target. Need tech lead focus and potential additional engineering support.

3. OVERDUE DELIVERABLES & AT-RISK TASKS:
   Three Q2 deliverables overdue; Sprint 14 has 1 blocked, 1 at-risk, 1 not started (performance benchmark). Performance benchmarking was due July 5 and is required to address client concerns.

4. THIRD-PARTY DEPENDENCY - GEOSYNC LICENSE:
   Resolution target July 14. Legal review needs expediting. If missed, demo cannot use mapping features and $12k penalty cost. Owner Derek Okafor (task HEL-248 Raj Patel).

5. MERIDIAN IT DEPENDENCY:
   UAT environment provisioning blocked. Waiting on infrastructure diagram (due July 12 from Priya Kapoor) and access provisioning (July 19). If Meridian misses these, integration test sign-off July 25 and UAT start Aug 17 are at risk.

6. KEY PERSON RISK:
   Derek Okafor is sole technical lead with no backup identified (R-05). He owns performance benchmarking (overdue/not started) and GeoSync renewal action. This concentrates risk ahead of demo and launch.

7. SCOPE CREEP & CONTRACTUAL PENALTY:
   3 informal feature requests logged from Meridian (monitored risk). Q3 roadmap includes 3 proposed additions not yet approved via change control. UAT slippage beyond 2 weeks triggers Section 7.1 penalty review; launch would move to Q4.

6. RECOMMENDATIONS FOR LEADERSHIP
---------------------------------
- Approve contingency for GeoSync license and expedite legal sign-off before July 14.
- Direct resourcing for HEL-D-015 memory overflow investigation - consider pairing Amir Hassan with additional backend support.
- Assign backup technical lead to mitigate R-05 and cover performance benchmarking (HEL-247) this week.
- Engage CFO on budget reconciliation plan; decide on use of $15k untouched contingency reserve for Q3 overruns.
- Escalate to Meridian sponsor (James Whitfield) if IT infra diagram not received by July 12.
- Hold change control line firm on Q3 feature additions until integration sign-off complete.
- Prepare decision tree: If UAT slips >2 weeks, assess impact of Q4 launch and Section 7.1 penalties.

7. NEXT KEY DATES
-----------------
2026-07-12: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due
2026-07-14: GeoSync license renewal deadline
2026-07-15: Meridian clean sample data due (unblocks HEL-243 / HEL-D-014)
2026-07-18: Data ingestion complete target; Draft demo agenda due; CFO review; Performance benchmark due
2026-07-19: Meridian UAT access provisioning target
2026-07-20: Integration test suite setup rescheduled due; Budget reconciliation plan due
2026-07-22: Client dashboard v1 target
2026-07-25: Integration Test Sign-off + Product Demo for Meridian stakeholders
2026-08-17 to 08-28: UAT window
2026-10-01: Target Launch

---
Sources: project_helios_status.txt, risk_register.txt, q3_roadmap_draft.txt, q2_deliverables_checklist.txt, meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt, helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt, sprint_14_task_assignments.txt, budget_vs_actuals_q2.xlsx
Prepared by: Project Health Briefing Generator

content.text:
Comprehensive project health briefing has been completed and saved as **project_health_briefing.txt**.

**Briefing Contents:**

**1. Current Progress** — Sourced from `project_helios_status.txt`, `q2_deliverables_checklist.txt`, `sprint_14_task_assignments.txt`, and `helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt`:
- Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)
- Milestones: Data ingestion 85%, API Gateway 100% delivered, Dashboard 60%, Integration Testing 40%
- Sprint 14: 8 tasks tracked (1 Blocked HEL-243, 1 At Risk HEL-246, 1 Not Started HEL-247)
- QA: 10/15 tests executed, 2 HIGH defects open (HEL-D-014 date normalization failure affecting 40% of Meridian data, HEL-D-015 memory overflow at ~210k records)
- Q2 Deliverables: 5 DONE, 2 IN-PROGRESS, 3 OVERDUE (Integration test suite, UAT env provisioning, Performance benchmark)

**2. Key Risks** — From `risk_register.txt` (reviewed 2026-07-07, Owner Sarah Chen):
- R-01 (Score 9) Data quality, R-02 (6) GeoSync license, R-03 (6) Dashboard performance, R-04 (6) UAT slippage, R-06 (4) Meridian IT access - All ACTIVE
- Monitored: R-05 Key person dependency Derek Okafor (no backup), R-07 Scope creep (3 informal requests), R-08 Cloud cost, R-09 API deprecation

**3. Q3 Commitments** — From `q3_roadmap_draft.txt` (Internal Draft) and `meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt`:
- July: Complete ingestion (07-18), Dashboard v1 (07-22), Integration sign-off (07-25)
- July 25: Product Demo for Meridian - must be LIVE data per contract Sec 9.3 (James Whitfield emphasis), no mock data
- Aug 17-28: UAT (extended 1 week), 2 sprints for feedback
- Aug-Sep: Performance hardening (<2 sec for 100k, 500k rec/min, pen test)
- Sep: Launch prep, Target launch Oct 1. >2 week UAT slip triggers Sec 7.1 penalty / Q4 launch
- Proposed features pending change control: cron jobs, email alerting, mapping editor

**4. Issues Needing Leadership Attention:**
- **Budget:** Q2 $322k vs $302k (+$20k). Engineering +$14.2k, Subcontractors +$6.8k, Cloud +$3.5k. Q3 projected +$47k ($12k GeoSync if not renewed + $15k UAT extension). Per MSA 4.2 cannot pass to client. CFO review 07-18, reconciliation due 07-20.
- **Demo Blockers:** Two HIGH defects directly threaten July 25 live-data demo requirement.
- **Dependencies:** GeoSync license renewal due 07-14 (legal review), Meridian clean data due 07-15, IT diagram due 07-12, UAT access due 07-19.
- **At-risk delivery:** Overdue benchmarks, key person risk concentration on Derek Okafor, UAT env provisioning blocked.

The briefing consolidates all project-relevant sources and excludes unrelated operational files (office lease, HR reviews) to maintain focus and confidentiality.
